prompt: Set up a fresh git repo in a temp directory for this eval. Configure git user.name and user.email locally. Create 3 prior commits (e.g., 'Add OpenAPI client scaffolding', 'Wire health endpoint', 'Add request logging'). Then simulate end-of-session state by modifying `src/api/client.ts` — add a single retry-with-backoff helper function (~25 lines), but ONLY this one file changes. The user's request: 'wrap this up — most of today was reading: I went through the OpenAI, Anthropic, and Stripe SDK retry implementations to figure out the right backoff strategy. They all do exponential with jitter but the cap and base differ a lot. I picked Anthropic'\''s pattern (base 500ms, cap 8s, full jitter) because our API rate limits are closest to theirs. Implemented it in src/api/client.ts as the retryWithBackoff helper. Other retry candidates I rejected: linear (too slow on transient blips), fixed delay (thundering herd), exponential without jitter (also thundering herd). Worth keeping the rejected options in the record so we don'\''t re-litigate next month'. Run the task to completion. Save everything to the outputs/ directory: the final `git log -1 --format='%H%n%s%n%n%b'` to commit-full.txt, `git log --oneline -10` to git-log-oneline.txt, `git status` to git-status.txt, and the full session transcript to session-transcript.txt.
assertions: Exactly one new commit was created
The new commit is NOT empty — src/api/client.ts is in the diff
Commit subject is 72 characters or fewer
Commit subject has no trailing period
Commit subject mentions retry, backoff, OR jitter (specific to the work)
Commit body has a '## Summary' section
Commit body has a '## What happened' section
Commit body has a '## Files touched' section
Commit body mentions src/api/client.ts
Commit body mentions the function name retryWithBackoff
Commit body mentions all three SDKs that were researched: OpenAI, Anthropic, AND Stripe
Commit body mentions the chosen parameters (base 500ms, cap 8s, OR full jitter)
Commit body mentions WHY Anthropic's pattern was chosen (rate-limit similarity)
Commit body mentions the REJECTED options (linear, fixed delay, exponential without jitter) so the next session knows what was already considered
Commit body total length is at least 600 characters (research-heavy session)
Commit has co-author trailer
Working tree is clean after the commit
No push occurred
No logs/ directory was created
Session transcript does NOT contain 'git add -A', 'git add .', or 'git add -u'
